title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for application metadata, transactions, events, and orphans and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for file metadata and object reconciliation state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 9140 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Orphan Repair.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Orphan Repair, keep Orphan Repair connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
